reasoning: Cigna's stock has shown strong short-term upward momentum, closing at 318.30 on March 28. This recent boost is supported by the completion of the $3.3 billion divestiture of its Medicare division to HCSC on March 19, 2025. Cigna announced the majority of these proceeds will go to share buybacks, which adds structural support and buying pressure to the stock in the near term. Additionally, the company recently raised its dividend to $1.51 per share. However, medium-to-long term headwinds remain as Cigna provided weak FY 2025 EPS guidance ($29.50 versus the consensus estimate of $33.26), which will cap upside potential as quarterly earnings are digested. Following instructions, short-term probabilities incorporate the buyback momentum (0.57), while uncertainty correctly increases (converging towards 0.5) over the longer horizons where fundamental earnings headwinds and general market volatility take over.
